entries.title: Monorepo inception — exxat-ds + studentUX submodules, workspace config
entries.what: Built the monorepo from scratch: exxat-ds and studentUX added as git submodules (read-only). Shared tsconfig.base.json with @exxat/ds/* and @exxat/student/* path aliases. pnpm-workspace.yaml, turbo.json. CLAUDE.md with full DS token table, component imports, and product map — loaded every session.
entries.why: Single source of truth for all Exxat product apps. DS submodules prevent divergence from the canonical design system. CLAUDE.md ensures every session starts with the same context.

entries.title: Exam Management scaffold — Admin + Student apps
entries.what: Scaffolded both apps from zero: Admin app (apps/exam-management/admin/) with sidebar, routes, DS imports. Student app (apps/exam-management/assessment-taker/) with assessment list, take-exam, and results pages. Both apps wired to Admin DS via @exxat/ds webpack alias.
entries.why: First product app in the monorepo. Established the pattern all other products follow: next.config.ts webpack alias, globals.css DS import, layout.tsx with Typekit + FA.

entries.title: Question Bank full redesign — new state shape, DS migration, folder tree
entries.what: Complete QB redesign: flattened folder tree, Saved/Draft status model (removed shortlisted), favorited questions, role-based filtering (My Questions default), DS Table migration (TableHeader/TableBody/TableRow/TableCell), sidebar search + DS DropdownMenu context menu, collaborator avatar stack, version history Popover, 14-column definition. Design spec written first.
entries.why: Original QB had raw HTML tables, non-DS buttons, and a state shape that couldn't support multi-folder questions or role-based views. Redesign aligned with Aarti's PRD §3 requirements.

entries.title: Assessment Builder + Courses page + QB column drag-reorder
entries.what: Assessment Builder: split layout, smart views, question picker, difficulty distribution chart, mini bars. Courses page: expandable table — course rows, offering rows, student counts. QB: column drag-reorder via HTML5 drag events, column header context menus (pin/hide/inline enum), subfolder folder mutations (rename, create inline, move, delete with impact popup), 5-level mock data.
entries.why: Assessment Builder is the core workflow connecting QB to assessments. Courses page is the entry point for faculty. Column drag-reorder was a direct Aarti directive.

entries.title: Governance layer — DS updates watcher, subagent telemetry, hooks cascade-check
entries.what: ds-updates watcher (Tier 2 swap-ready architecture): scans DS submodule deltas, maps to product code. Subagent telemetry wired to architect-input.py. Hooks: cascade-check on fix/redesign prompts closes Pattern B (sibling coverage). claude-updates watcher auto-invoked on session start. Appearances toggle (theme switcher) shipped in PCE + exam-management admin.
entries.why: DS submodule updates were silently breaking product code. Subagent telemetry enables the architect agent to propose rules based on evidence. Appearances toggle: Aarti needs to preview both Exxat One and Prism branding.

entries.title: QB multi-column sort, sidebar polish, WCAG 400% zoom compliance
entries.what: Multi-column sort (Item 8). Sidebar: Active/Inactive course grouping, ancestor path in hover popover, deep subfolder mock data. WCAG 400% zoom: toolbar layout reflow, sticky positioning fix, title consolidation — all WCAG 1.4.10 Reflow compliant. 200% zoom compact title bar.
entries.why: WCAG 400% compliance is non-negotiable for government/healthcare contracts. Multi-column sort was a backlog item from Aarti's May 7 session.

entries.title: QB question actions — archive/restore/copy, bulk bar, status filter, folder pin
entries.what: Question actions: archive, restore, copy to/remove from/multi-location. Bulk action bar: compact icon-only folder actions with tooltips, Radix nested-modal conflict resolved. Status filter with counts. All Questions location. Folder pin (thumbtack). Lock icon for private folders. Role-based access: exam admin gets institution-wide folder visibility including private.
entries.why: Archive/restore/copy were the last PRD §4.6 gaps. Bulk bar enables faculty to manage large question sets efficiently. Lock icons surface visibility rules without a tooltip.

entries.title: QB completion — full-text search, toast + undo, row actions pill, location lock icons
entries.what: Full-text search across all question fields. Dark action toast with progress bar, undo, and folder link — on all actions. Row actions pill. Location lock icons (private folder indicator). Pinned section in sidebar below search. Context-aware toast copy per action type.
entries.why: Full-text search was the last major missing QB feature. Toast+undo pattern matches PRD §4.6 reversibility requirement. Sidebar pinned section surfaced most-used folders.

entries.title: Exxat Prism Portal — workspace hub with all 6 products
entries.what: Built apps/portal/ from zero: AppSidebar (Prism wordmark, Apps nav, RS footer), SiteHeader with SidebarTrigger, WorkspacePage with product card grid, ProductCard with Open Admin button + ⋯ dropdown. 4 layout variants: Gradient / Bento / Dark / Rows. Per-product SVG illustrations. Adobe-style card variant. Connector list + App Store detail page. Deployed to Vercel at port 3100.
entries.why: Single entry point to all Exxat products. Romit and Arun need a launcher that looks production-ready for stakeholder demos. Prism theme aligns with the new brand direction.

entries.title: BUILD-STATUS.md — session continuity reference for all apps
entries.what: Created docs/BUILD-STATUS.md: per-product build status (what's built, what's not done yet, what's blocked) so every new session starts with accurate context without reading code. Covers Exam Management, PCE, Portal, and planned products.
entries.why: Context compaction was causing sessions to start cold — not knowing what was already built and what was in-progress. BUILD-STATUS.md is the first thing a new session reads.

entries.title: PRD Watcher — daily SharePoint sync with auto-apply + flag
entries.what: Scheduled agent (prd-watcher-daily, 9am EDT) reads Monil's PCE PRD and Nipun's Exam Mgmt roadmap + PRD via M365 MCP. Diffs against snapshots. Applies clear changes to code; flags ambiguous ones to docs/watch/flags/. Registry at docs/watch/registry.json. 4 docs registered.
entries.why: Prevent design drift — when Monil or Nipun updates a PRD, code should reflect it by next morning without Romit having to prompt.

entries.title: Compliance sweep — weekly FERPA/HIPAA/WCAG audit across all product TSX
entries.what: Scheduled agent (compliance-sweep-weekly, Mon 8am EDT) greps all product TSX files for 12 rule violations: FERPA student-ID coexistence, WCAG opacity-60-on-text, missing aria-hidden on FA icons, raw table usage, toast usage, missing DataTable empty state, and more. Writes violation-inventory.json and compliance-report.md. P1/P2/P3 severity with consequence language.
entries.why: FERPA violation = $43K–$274K fine per incident. WCAG failures block government contracts. Audit must run automatically — not only when Romit notices a problem.

entries.title: PCE + Exam Management UI patterns docs — session-start governance references
entries.what: apps/pce/docs/patterns/pce-ui-patterns.md (329 lines) and apps/exam-management/docs/patterns/ui-patterns.md (529 lines): page shell anatomy, DataTable conventions, interaction patterns, empty state formula, DS component map, token conventions, WCAG 2.1 AA checklist, FERPA §99.31, HIPAA §164.514. Auto-loaded at session start via CLAUDE.md.
entries.why: Without these docs, every session re-discovers the same violations — wrong DataTable pattern, raw buttons, missing aria-hidden. Loading them at start eliminates the correction loop.

entries.title: Doc Watcher UI — paste SharePoint links, validate duplicates, registry management
entries.what: Next.js app at tools/doc-watcher/ (localhost:3002, deployed to Vercel). Registry page: paste one or many SharePoint URLs, Check Links validates duplicates, Add prompts for product + label. Updates page: filterable feed of all architecture changes, PRD updates, compliance findings — grouped by date, P1 entries red-bordered. GitHub API backend — no database.
entries.why: Romit needed a simple interface to register new docs without editing JSON by hand. Also a cross-reference surface to show stakeholders (Arun, Nipun, Monil) what changed and why.

entries.title: DS snapshot — 44-component API surface, eliminates per-session DS source reads
entries.what: Python script (scripts/build_ds_snapshot.py) reads exxat-ds/packages/ui/src/, extracts variants, sizes, and key props via regex into docs/watch/ds-snapshot.json (44 components). Compliance-reviewer now reads snapshot in Step 0 instead of full source files. Daily refresh agent (ds-snapshot-refresh, 10am EDT).
entries.why: Reading full DS source per session cost ~4K tokens/component. Snapshot reduces it to ~50 tokens/grep — 80× reduction in DS lookup cost per session.

entries.title: Stakeholder decision cache — 6 PCE decisions + weekly Granola transcript extraction
entries.what: docs/watch/stakeholder-decisions.json seeded with 6 confirmed PCE PRD decisions (Monil): program-level scope, clinical CE focus, anonymous responses, N=5 threshold, 1-5 Likert enforced, 7-year retention. PRD watcher Step 6 extracts new decisions on every sync. Granola intake agent (Mon 9am EDT) pulls 7-day raw transcripts and extracts Aarti/Vishaka directives.
entries.why: Eliminates re-reading PRDs + transcripts every session to answer 'what did Aarti confirm about X'. Queryable JSON, transcript-level granularity — not synthesised summaries.

entries.title: Transcript→PRD diff — weekly P1 flag when Aarti directive contradicts PRD
entries.what: Scheduled agent (transcript-prd-diff-weekly, Tue 9am EDT) reads 14 days of Granola transcripts, extracts directives from Aarti/Adi/Vishaka, cross-references against PRD snapshots. Any contradiction flagged P1 with exact transcript quote + contradicting PRD text side by side.
entries.why: The cohort-filter incident: Aarti's 'no filters' directive was in the raw transcript but not in the summary — a filter was built and removed after two verification cycles. This agent catches that error class automatically.

entries.title: BUILD-STATUS auto-sync from Nipun's Excel roadmap
entries.what: PRD watcher Steps 7a–7f: when exam-roadmap-nipun Excel is processed, extract project status grid, map Excel status values (Completed→🟢 Shipped, In Progress→🟡 In Progress, Not Started→⚪ Not started), update docs/BUILD-STATUS.md Exam Management section inline. No manual updates needed.
entries.why: BUILD-STATUS.md was going stale — Nipun marks items complete in Excel but the doc required a manual update. Now syncs every morning.

entries.title: BUILD-STATUS updated from Nipun roadmap Excel
entries.what: Added feature status table to Exam Management section: Create and Manage Question Bank → 🟢 Shipped, My Questions & All Questions → 🟢 Shipped, Assessment Creation → 🟡 In Progress, In Assessment Experience → 🟡 In Progress, Course Offering Perspective → 🟡 In Progress, Question Creation (Manual & AI) → 🟡 In Progress, Question Tagging/Import → 🟡 In Progress, Assessment Distribution → 🟡 In Progress, Dashboard → 🟡 In Progress. PCE quick-orientation status updated to 🟡 In Progress — target 30 Aug 2026. Last updated date bumped to 2026-05-19.
entries.why: Nipun's roadmap Excel synced for first time — status values applied to BUILD-STATUS.md. PCE Phase 1 target date (30 Aug 2026) confirmed from Monil's PRD.

entries.title: BUILD-STATUS updated from Nipun roadmap Excel
entries.what: Added two new feature rows: 'Setup — Logo, LMS integration, RBAC, Import Courses' → 🟡 In Progress; 'Base entities — Faculty, Students' → 🟡 In Progress. Last updated date bumped to 2026-05-23.
entries.why: Nipun's roadmap Excel updated — two new work items appeared that were not previously tracked in BUILD-STATUS.

entries.title: PCE PRD — Distribute Survey wizard steps now defined (5-step structure)
entries.what: Step 2 Distribute Survey now has explicit 5-step wizard: Properties/Details, Distribution, Survey Design, Communication, Report Access.
entries.why: CLEAR design decision — this will drive the wizard step structure in the PCE admin app when Distribute Survey is built.

entries.title: PCE PRD — Shared Platform Architecture section added under Analytics
entries.what: New section: Shared Platform Architecture (Between PCE and General Surveys): Survey type, Template engine, Distribution engine, Analytics foundation.
entries.why: AMBIGUOUS — implications for whether PCE and General Surveys share data models or just UI patterns. Needs clarification from Monil.

entries.title: PCE PRD — Limitation of Current Survey Capability section added
entries.what: New section documenting 3 limitations of current Exxat Surveys: no bulk push, auto-render sections, access control within form.
entries.why: Non-functional context addition explaining redesign rationale. No code action needed.

entries.title: PCE PRD — Dependency renamed: SIS/course roster → SIS/LMS
entries.what: Dependencies table: 'SIS/course roster integration' renamed to 'SIS/LMS'; risk text changed from 'Surveys cannot auto-trigger; manual workaround required for Phase 1' to 'Survey distribution cannot be automated'.
entries.why: CLEAR — scope of dependency broadened. Informational.

entries.title: Exam Roadmap — 'Question bank on screen analytics' removed from Excel
entries.what: Feature that was previously listed as TBD is no longer present in Nipun's Excel. Never reached BUILD-STATUS (was TBD → skipped).
entries.why: CLEAR removal — possible descope or deferral. Confirm with Nipun.

entries.title: Exam Roadmap — Stakeholder Alignment column added; Question Creation has open alignment points
entries.what: New 'Stakeholder Allignment' column in Excel. Question creation (Manual & AI) marked with 'Alignment Points' — suggests open stakeholder disagreements.
entries.why: AMBIGUOUS — unknown what alignment gaps exist for Question Creation.

entries.title: PCE PRD — Decision extracted: Survey/CE co-existence redesign approach (pce-decision-007)
entries.what: New decision: The overall approach is to re-design Surveys so that Course Evaluation and Survey can co-exist.

entries.title: PCE PRD — Decision extracted: Three new constructs (pce-decision-008)
entries.what: New decision: Introduce Survey type, Course type, and Subject as three new constructs to enable CE distribution.

entries.title: PCE PRD — Decision extracted: Track submission only, not responses (pce-decision-009) — FERPA implication
entries.what: New decision: Track submission only, not response content. FERPA implication: student responses must remain untraceable.

entries.title: __updates() dev console — daily PRD + compliance feed in browser DevTools
entries.what: window.__updates() registered in both pce and exam-management admin apps. Auto-prints P1/prd-change summary on page load (dev only, tree-shaken in prod). Supports filters: __updates('today'), __updates('pce'), __updates('corrections'), __updates('p1'), __updates('week').
entries.why: Romit can cross-reference design decisions in the browser while building — see what changed today without switching tabs. Zero product UI pollution.
